title: Azure Network Security Group (NSG) firewall-rule posture
description: Per-NSG network security-rule posture captured read-only from Azure Resource Manager (ARM Reader role): the inbound/outbound security rules (name, direction, access allow/deny, protocol, source/destination address-prefix, source/destination port range, priority) and a summary count of subnet / network-interface associations. The minimum that demonstrates the network-segmentation control (e.g. no NSG allows 0.0.0.0/0 inbound on management ports 22/3389). RULE configuration only — NEVER flow logs, packet captures, traffic contents, secrets, or PII; the connector never mutates a network resource (read-only list). The platform evaluator decides pass/fail per (control, NSG); the connector emits a descriptive default verdict. x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9).

nsg_id.description: ARM resource id of the network security group.

associated_subnets.description: Count of subnet associations (summary, not topology).

associated_nics.description: Count of network-interface associations (summary, not topology).

rules.description: The NSG's operator-authored inbound/outbound security rules (platform default rules excluded). RULE metadata only.

direction.description: Traffic direction the rule applies to.

access.description: Whether the rule allows or denies the matched traffic.

protocol.description: Protocol the rule matches (tcp | udp | icmp | * ).

source_address_prefix.description: Source address prefix / tag the rule matches (e.g. 203.0.113.0/24, Internet, *).

destination_address_prefix.description: Destination address prefix / tag the rule matches.

source_port_range.description: Source port range the rule matches.

destination_port_range.description: Destination port range the rule matches (e.g. 22, 0-65535, *).
